overall_assessment.primary_concern: Three mega-files (app.rs, editor_state.rs, bridge.rs) violate functional paradigm by aggregating too many responsibilities. Structural coupling between layers undermines modularity.

refactoring_recommendations.title: Extract InputStateMachine from app.rs (lines 379-1000)
refactoring_recommendations.reason: handle_key_event (~605 lines) contains 12+ distinct InputState branches, count accumulation, self-insert dispatch, and motion handling. Single function violates SRP and functional composition principles.

proposed_solution.method_signature: pub fn process_key(machine_state: InputState, key: KeyEvent, editor: &mut EditorState) -> (InputState, DeferredAction)
benefits: Pure function: InputState + KeyEvent -> (InputState, DeferredAction)
Eliminates mutable borrow of app.rs's App struct
Enables property-based testing of state machine transitions
Separates terminal I/O from domain logic

steps: Create crates/alfred-tui/src/input_machine.rs
Extract InputState enum (already defined) to new module
Extract handle_key_event as pure function: process_key(...) -> (InputState, DeferredAction)
Extract sub-handlers: process_normal_mode, process_insert_mode, process_visual_mode, process_command_mode
Replace app.rs handle_key_event with call to input_machine::process_key
Update app.rs main event loop to assign returned (InputState, DeferredAction)
Test: all existing key handling tests pass unchanged
refactoring_recommendations.testing_strategy: property-based: generate random KeyEvent sequences, verify InputState invariants (no invalid transitions, count accumulation matches expectations)
risk_mitigation: Keep original handle_key_event as temporary thin wrapper during transition
Add integration tests comparing old/new behavior on 50+ key sequences
Commit incrementally per InputState variant extraction
refactoring_recommendations.impact_on_architecture: Moves input handling from imperative-shell boundary toward functional-core

refactoring_recommendations.title: Extract ColonCommandDispatcher from app.rs (lines 984-1150)
refactoring_recommendations.fowler_technique: Replace Conditional with Polymorphism / Introduce Command Pattern
refactoring_recommendations.reason: execute_colon_command uses monolithic match over 10+ command strings (q, q!, wq, w, e, eval, g/, g!/, v/, s/, %s/). Each command reaches 5-8 lines into alfred_core, violating DIP (Dependency Inversion Principle). Brittle to extension.

current_state.problem: Each branch directly accesses buffer, cursor, viewport via alfred_core:: paths

proposed_solution.structure: pub struct ColonCommand { name: String, handler: Box<dyn Fn(&mut EditorState, &str) -> CommandResult> }
benefits: Open/Closed Principle: adding new colon commands requires no modification to dispatcher
Commands become data-driven, not code-driven
Each command handler is testable in isolation
Dependency Injection: editor state passed via handler function, not global coupling

steps: Create crates/alfred-tui/src/colon_commands.rs
Define ColonCommand type with name and handler closure
Extract each match arm into dedicated function: cmd_quit(), cmd_write(), cmd_eval(), cmd_global_delete(), cmd_substitute()
Create builder function populate_builtin_colon_commands(registry: &mut HashMap<String, ColonCommand>)
Replace execute_colon_command with registry lookup and dispatch
Update App struct to hold colon_commands registry (initialized in new())
Tests: verify all 11+ commands execute correctly via registry
risk_mitigation: Registry is initialized once in App::new(), immutable thereafter
Handler closures can still access shared EditorState via Rc<RefCell<>>
Keep original execute_colon_command as deprecated wrapper during migration
refactoring_recommendations.impact_on_architecture: Enables plugin-friendly colon command system; reduces coupling to app.rs

refactoring_recommendations.title: Extract CommandDispatcher facade from app.rs
refactoring_recommendations.reason: 355 occurrences of alfred_core:: fully-qualified paths in app.rs indicate direct coupling to all core submodules. TUI should communicate with editor via a single facade, not reach into internals.

current_state.violation: TUI violates Dependency Inversion Principle (depends on concrete modules, not abstraction)

methods: pub fn set_buffer_content(&mut self, content: String)
pub fn move_cursor_by(&mut self, direction: Direction, count: usize)
pub fn insert_char(&mut self, ch: char)
pub fn set_message(&mut self, msg: String)
pub fn undo(&mut self) -> Result<(), String>
pub fn redo(&mut self) -> Result<(), String>
proposed_solution.implementation: EditorFacade wraps Rc<RefCell<EditorState>>, provides controlled read/write access without exposing internals

steps: Create crates/alfred-core/src/facade.rs with EditorFacade struct
Implement all read methods as forwarding to wrapped EditorState fields
Implement all mutation methods as controlled edits (with invariant checks)
Export EditorFacade from alfred-core lib.rs
Replace all 355 alfred_core:: references in app.rs with EditorFacade method calls
Add compile-time assertion (cfg_attr) that alfred-tui only imports EditorFacade from alfred_core (catches new direct imports)
Tests: verify facade behavior matches previous direct access
refactoring_recommendations.testing_strategy: regression tests comparing old/new behavior on cursor, buffer, viewport, register operations
risk_mitigation: Facade is additive (wraps existing code), no breaking changes to EditorState internals
Each facade method is thin wrapper, zero logic change
Can stage migration method-by-method
refactoring_recommendations.impact_on_architecture: Eliminates structural coupling; enables substitutable EditorState implementations (testing, mocking)

refactoring_recommendations.title: Extract CommandRegistry dispatcher from app.rs
refactoring_recommendations.reason: execute_deferred_action dispatches built-in, Lisp dynamic, and macro commands via repeated pattern: command::execute(). Add new deferred action type = modify app.rs. Violates SRP.

current_state.problem: Each new deferred action type requires modifying app.rs event loop

proposed_solution.method: pub fn execute(state: &mut EditorState, action: DeferredAction, runtime: &LispRuntime) -> Result<(), String>
benefits: Pure function: DeferredAction + EditorState -> EditorState'
App.rs becomes thin event loop, not command processor
New command types added without modifying app.rs
Enables composition (chain multiple deferred actions)

steps: Create crates/alfred-core/src/command_dispatcher.rs
Move execute_deferred_action logic from app.rs to new module
Accept LispRuntime as parameter instead of accessing from App
Replace app.rs execute_deferred_action call with module function
Update DeferredAction enum to support new variants without app.rs changes
Test: all command execution paths (built-in, Lisp, macro, search) pass unchanged
refactoring_recommendations.testing_strategy: property-based: generate random DeferredAction sequences, verify idempotency and state consistency
risk_mitigation: All command execution logic moves unchanged; no behavior modification
App.rs only changes to remove execute_deferred_action implementation (thin wrapper remains)
refactoring_recommendations.impact_on_architecture: App.rs becomes event loop + renderer; commands are pure domain logic

refactoring_recommendations.title: Decompose EditorState into subsystem types
refactoring_recommendations.reason: EditorState has 39+ public fields aggregating: buffer, cursor, viewport, keymaps, registers, undo/redo, macros, marks, jump list, change list, search, theme, panels, focus. Single mutable container prevents composition and violates functional paradigm.

current_state.problem: Each subsystem modification requires updating new() and snapshot/undo logic

proposed_solution.approach: Extract 6 subsystem types, keep EditorState as composition root
subsystem_types: RegisterSet { registers: HashMap<char, RegisterEntry>, pending: Option<char> }
SearchState { pattern: Option<String>, forward: bool, last_char_find: Option<(CharFindKind, char)> }
ModeState { mode: String, keymaps: HashMap<String, Keymap>, active_keymaps: Vec<String>, cursor_shapes: HashMap<String, String> }
proposed_solution.refactored_editor_state: pub struct EditorState { buffer: BufferView, cursor: CursorView, registers: RegisterSet, undo: UndoState, search: SearchState, mode: ModeState, theme: Theme, named_themes: HashMap<String, Theme>, panels: PanelRegistry, panel_focus: PanelFocus, commands: CommandRegistry, hooks: HookRegistry, message: Option<String>, running: bool }
benefits: Functional composition: each subsystem can be updated independently
Testability: subsystems have focused, clear contracts
Future extensibility: new subsystems don't touch existing ones
Snapshotting: UndoState can implement Copy semantics once fields are smaller

steps: Define subsystem types in new modules: crates/alfred-core/src/buffer_view.rs, cursor_view.rs, register_set.rs, etc.
Add pub field accessors to EditorState (buffer_view(), cursor_view(), registers(), etc.)
Gradually migrate register_builtin_commands from direct field access to subsystem accessors
Extract snapshot/undo logic: impl UndoState { fn snapshot(&self) -> UndoSnapshot { ... } }
Update App to use subsystem getters instead of direct EditorState fields
Migrate bridge.rs primitives to use subsystem types (e.g., buffer-insert accesses buffer_view.buffer)
Test: all existing tests pass; new subsystem types have focused unit tests
refactoring_recommendations.testing_strategy: unit tests per subsystem (CursorView::move_up_viewport_adjusted, RegisterSet::set_with_fallback, etc.) plus integration tests for EditorState composition
risk_mitigation: Subsystem types are internal; EditorState API remains unchanged
Add deprecation period where old direct field access still works (via pub fields)
Migrate register_builtin_commands incrementally, command by command
Keep undo_stack/redo_stack as Vec<(BufferView, CursorView)> (minimal snapshot) until subsystems stabilize
refactoring_recommendations.impact_on_architecture: Transforms EditorState from monolithic to compositional; enables future subsystem independence

refactoring_recommendations.title: Refactor register_builtin_commands from procedural to data-driven
refactoring_recommendations.fowler_technique: Replace Method with Method Object / Introduce Table-Driven Pattern
refactoring_recommendations.reason: register_builtin_commands is ~887 lines (lines 258-1145), registering 30+ commands inline using repeated crate:: prefixes. Violates DRY and makes command set opaque. Should be declarative table.

current_state.problem: Each command registration is 5-30 lines of boilerplate; same pattern repeated 30+ times

proposed_solution.data_structure: Vec<CommandDefinition> where CommandDefinition = { name: &'static str, handler: fn(&mut EditorState) -> Result<(), String> }
proposed_solution.refactored_signature: pub fn register_builtin_commands(state: &mut EditorState) { for cmd in BUILTIN_COMMANDS.iter() { register_command(state, cmd); } }
proposed_solution.data_table: const BUILTIN_COMMANDS: &[CommandDefinition] = &[ CommandDefinition { name: "cursor-up", handler: handlers::cursor_up }, CommandDefinition { name: "cursor-down", handler: handlers::cursor_down }, ... ]
benefits: Command registry becomes declarative, not procedural
Handlers move to separate handlers module (crates/alfred-core/src/handlers/mod.rs)
Can generate command list/docs from table
Easy to disable/enable commands via feature flags
New command = add row to table + define handler

steps: Create crates/alfred-core/src/handlers/ directory with handler modules (cursor.rs, editing.rs, register.rs, search.rs, etc.)
Define CommandDefinition type in crates/alfred-core/src/command_table.rs
Extract each command handler from register_builtin_commands into handlers/ module
Create BUILTIN_COMMANDS table in command_table.rs (initially 30+ rows)
Refactor register_builtin_commands to loop over BUILTIN_COMMANDS
Add new pub fn command_list() -> Vec<&'static str> for documentation
Test: verify all 30+ commands still register and execute correctly
refactoring_recommendations.testing_strategy: parametrized tests iterating over BUILTIN_COMMANDS, verifying each command executes without error
risk_mitigation: Handlers are extracted unchanged (no logic modification)
Table structure is additive (no breaking changes to CommandRegistry)
Can keep original register_builtin_commands as temporary wrapper during migration
refactoring_recommendations.impact_on_architecture: Register_builtin_commands shrinks from 887 to <100 lines; handlers become modular and testable

refactoring_recommendations.title: Extract BridgePrimitives subsystem from bridge.rs
refactoring_recommendations.reason: 5198-line bridge.rs directly accesses 8+ alfred_core submodules via repeated Rc<RefCell<EditorState>> borrows (buffer, cursor, viewport, hook, command, theme, panel, editor_state). Creates intense structural coupling; 100+ direct field accesses per primitive.

current_state.pattern: Each primitive borrows state, accesses 3-8 submodule functions, mutates EditorState fields

proposed_solution.approach: Leverage EditorFacade (from refactoring #3) as single bridge to core
proposed_solution.new_structure: bridge.rs uses only EditorFacade methods, never directly accesses submodules
proposed_solution.refactored_primitive: fn register_buffer_insert(env: &Rc<RefCell<Env>>, facade: EditorFacade) { define_native_closure(env, "buffer-insert", move |_env, args| { let text = extract_string_arg(&args, "buffer-insert")?; facade.insert_char(&text)?; Ok(Value::Nil) }) }
benefits: Bridge only imports alfred_core::facade::EditorFacade (one import instead of eight)
Eliminates all direct submodule access patterns
Makes coupling explicit: bridge->facade->submodules (three-layer, not flat)
Enables testing: facade can be mocked for unit testing primitives

steps: Refactor (with refactoring #3) to ensure EditorFacade has all methods bridge primitives need
Remove all alfred_core::buffer, alfred_core::cursor, etc. imports from bridge.rs
Replace all direct submodule calls with EditorFacade method calls
Primitive signatures change: register_buffer_insert(env: &Rc<RefCell<Env>>, state: Rc<RefCell<EditorState>>) -> register_buffer_insert(env: &Rc<RefCell<Env>>, facade: EditorFacade)
Update all 35 primitive registrations in register_core_primitives()
Test: all Lisp tests pass; primitives behave identically
refactoring_recommendations.testing_strategy: snapshot tests comparing Lisp runtime output before/after; property-based tests on primitive behavior
risk_mitigation: EditorFacade is thin wrapper (no behavior change)
Primitive behavior unchanged (only access path changes)
Keep original primitive implementations as fallback during transition
refactoring_recommendations.impact_on_architecture: Reduces bridge coupling; positions EditorFacade as canonical abstraction boundary

refactoring_recommendations.title: Extract RendererModule from app.rs renderer operations
refactoring_recommendations.reason: Main event loop in app.rs intersperses rendering logic (parse color themes, gutter rendering, buffer display) with command execution. Renderer is separate module but app.rs still reaches into its internals.

current_state.concern: Renderer and App are tightly coupled; app.rs must know about renderer's internal structure to trigger redraws

methods: pub fn render_frame(&mut self, editor: &EditorState, lisp_runtime: &LispRuntime) -> io::Result<()>
proposed_solution.implementation: Facade owns Terminal, SyntaxHighlighter, and all renderer state; app.rs calls only these three methods

steps: Create RendererFacade in crates/alfred-tui/src/renderer_facade.rs
Move renderer ownership to RendererFacade
Replace all inline renderer calls in app.rs event loop with RendererFacade method calls
Simplify app.rs event loop: read key -> process key -> render frame -> repeat
refactoring_recommendations.testing_strategy: integration tests with mock terminal backend, verify render calls synchronize with editor state changes
risk_mitigation: Renderer logic unchanged (only facade layer added)
Existing renderer tests continue to pass
refactoring_recommendations.impact_on_architecture: App.rs becomes pure event loop; renderer becomes black box

refactoring_recommendations.title: Fix dead code: PluginRegistry in main.rs, dead assignment in bridge.rs
refactoring_recommendations.reason: Two low-risk dead code removals: (1) PluginRegistry built but dropped in main.rs (let _ = reg); (2) let _lisp_env = runtime.env() assigned but unused in bridge.rs

current_state.main_rs_issue: let _ = reg = PluginRegistry::load_plugins(...) immediately dropped; noted as future work but creates confusion
current_state.bridge_rs_issue: let _lisp_env = runtime.env() in register_define_command, marked as unused

proposed_solution.main_rs_fix: Remove PluginRegistry::load_plugins() call entirely; add TODO comment explaining Lisp plugins are now primary extension mechanism
proposed_solution.bridge_rs_fix: Remove unused let _lisp_env assignment; if env is genuinely needed, use it; otherwise delete

steps: Remove let _ = PluginRegistry::load_plugins(...) from main.rs
Remove PluginRegistry import from main.rs if no longer used
Remove let _lisp_env assignment from register_define_command
Verify no other code path expects PluginRegistry (grep codebase)
Test: e2e tests pass; no plugin loading behavior change (Lisp plugins already loaded)
refactoring_recommendations.testing_strategy: smoke test: editor starts, loads Lisp plugins from disk, functions normally
risk_mitigation: Both removals are purely dead code (no behavior change)
PluginRegistry remains available for future Rust plugin system
refactoring_recommendations.impact_on_architecture: Minor code cleanliness; clarifies that Lisp plugins are primary extension point

refactoring_recommendations.title: Replace boolean blindness: execute_global_delete invert parameter
refactoring_recommendations.reason: execute_global_delete uses plain bool invert parameter. A small enum (MatchingLines / NonMatchingLines) would be clearer and prevent accidental boolean inversion bugs.

current_state.function_signature: fn execute_global_delete(state: &mut EditorState, pattern: &str, invert: bool) -> (InputState, DeferredAction)
current_state.problem: Caller must reason about bool semantics; easy to confuse invert=true as 'delete inverted matches' vs 'invert the matching logic'

proposed_solution.refactored_signature: fn execute_global_delete(state: &mut EditorState, pattern: &str, mode: GlobalDeleteMode) -> (InputState, DeferredAction)
proposed_solution.call_sites: execute_global_delete(state, pattern, GlobalDeleteMode::MatchingLines) vs execute_global_delete(state, pattern, GlobalDeleteMode::NonMatchingLines)

steps: Define GlobalDeleteMode enum in app.rs (or editor_state.rs)
Update execute_global_delete signature to use GlobalDeleteMode
Update all call sites (g/pattern/d -> MatchingLines; g!/pattern/d -> NonMatchingLines)
Test: all global delete tests pass with new enum
refactoring_recommendations.testing_strategy: property-based tests with both enum variants, verify correct lines deleted
risk_mitigation: Enum change is API-only (no logic modification)
All existing tests can be updated mechanically
refactoring_recommendations.impact_on_architecture: Minor; improves code clarity and reduces bugs from boolean confusion

refactoring_recommendations.title: Extract duplicate range-computation logic (delete/yank operator patterns)
refactoring_recommendations.reason: execute_delete_with_motion and execute_yank_with_motion both share CharWise/LineWise pattern with identical range-computation logic. Should be factored into shared helper.

proposed_solution.new_function: fn compute_operator_range(text_object: &TextObject, cursor: Cursor, buffer: &Buffer) -> Range
proposed_solution.refactored_delete: fn execute_delete_with_motion(...) { let range = compute_operator_range(...); let text = extract_text(&buffer, range); delete_text(&mut buffer, range); set_register(..., text); }
proposed_solution.refactored_yank: fn execute_yank_with_motion(...) { let range = compute_operator_range(...); let text = extract_text(&buffer, range); set_register(..., text); }

steps: Extract compute_operator_range() helper function
Replace duplicated range logic in execute_delete_with_motion with call to helper
Replace duplicated range logic in execute_yank_with_motion with call to helper
Test: all delete/yank tests pass unchanged
refactoring_recommendations.testing_strategy: property-based tests on compute_operator_range with various TextObject/Cursor combinations
risk_mitigation: New function is pure (cursor, buffer -> range)
Behavior unchanged (only refactoring)

implementation_sequence.name: Foundation: Abstraction Boundaries
implementation_sequence.goal: Establish EditorFacade and eliminate direct structural coupling between layers

implementation_sequence.name: TUI Decomposition
implementation_sequence.goal: Extract input state machine, command dispatchers, and renderer facade from app.rs

implementation_sequence.name: Core Refactoring
implementation_sequence.goal: Decompose EditorState into subsystems; make command registration data-driven

implementation_sequence.name: Code Cleanliness
implementation_sequence.goal: Remove dead code, fix boolean blindness, deduplicate logic

refactoring_3_must_precede: EditorFacade must exist before bridge.rs can use it
refactoring_1_must_precede: InputStateMachine extraction makes col-command handling clearer
refactoring_5_depends_on: Subsystem extraction easier with EditorFacade reducing coupling
refactoring_6_depends_on: Data-driven command table works better with subsystem types

risk_assessment_summary.mitigation_strategy: Phase 1 (abstraction boundaries) must complete before later phases; use feature flags to gradually enable new code paths; commit incremental progress after each extracted component

architectural_principles_addressed.SRP: Each file/module has one reason to change
architectural_principles_addressed.OCP: New commands via table extension, not code modification
architectural_principles_addressed.DIP: TUI depends on EditorFacade (abstraction), not concrete modules
architectural_principles_addressed.functional_paradigm: Pure core (command handlers, input machine, command dispatcher) with I/O shell (app.rs event loop, renderer facade)
